author: y4rr
content: Closed via #671 with corrected root cause. My original hypothesis (the [sdata + %off] addressing pattern) was WRONG — the real bug was a register-name collision. The user-declared %tid register clashes with PTX's special %tid.x/y/z special registers; ptxas can't disambiguate. Fixed by renaming %tid → %my_tid file-wide (2 replace_all edits affecting 12 kernels — not just the 6 reductions). The %sbase/%saddr address rewrites I did earlier (in #668, B2, and my own manual experiments) are functionally correct but were chasing the wrong bug. After the %tid rename, all 27 previously-failing tests PASS. Workspace builds clean. The 6 #[ignore = 'requires CUDA device'] from issue #666 were also reverted — those tests now run and pass. Lesson learned: hypothesis-driven debugging without proof is rust-fix-discipline Step 1.6 territory; should have asked for ptxas earlier instead of trying to fix-and-test for 4 rounds.
